item.title: Event Wagers Face $143 Million Insider Problem as War Bets Boom
item.description: Early last Monday, a burst of concentrated trades set off a week in which Wall Street and Washington were abuzz about the possibility that insiders have been using early knowledge of President Donald Trump’s decision making on Iran to lock in big profits.

item.title: Hormuz Tracker: Saudi Crude Heads to Pakistan in Rare Transit
item.description: A small cargo of Saudi crude is heading to Pakistan after crossing the Strait of Hormuz along a route hugging the Iranian coastline. A total of seven vessels were visible leaving the Persian Gulf on Saturday.

item.title: Emerging Markets Rout Lures Contrarians Betting on Rate Cuts
item.description: With emerging markets on the brink of their worst month since 2022, TT International and AllianceBernstein are making a bold wager that this is a good time to buy.

item.title: Apple Pivots Its AI Strategy to App Store, Search-Like Platform Approach
item.description: Also: The company finally kills the Mac Pro.

item.title: Telecom Italia Scraps Plan to Renew Inwit Tower Contract in 2030
item.description: Telecom Italia SpA said it won’t renew a mobile-phone tower agreement with Inwit SpA that would have extended the contract through 2038.

item.title: Syria Eyes $1 Billion Revival for Postal and Mobile Networks
item.description: Syria is seeking foreign investors for a $1 billion drive to revamp its mobile-phone coverage and postal service, as the war-torn country rebuilds after the US lifted sanctions.

item.title: Banks Urge RBI to Relax New Rules as $30 Billion Unwinding Looms
item.description: Lenders in India are urging the central bank to relax tighter rules for foreign-exchange transactions, which came as bets that could exert strain on the rupee climbed to at least $30 billion, according to people familiar with the developments.

item.title: Strikes Continue as Houthis Join Iran War and US Troops Arrive
item.description: Attacks in the Middle East conflict extended into a fifth week Sunday, with Israel striking Tehran and Saudi Arabia intercepting almost a dozen drones, a day after Yemen-based Houthi militants entered the war.

item.title: China Summons US Envoy to HK Over Alert on Security Law Changes
item.description: Beijing summoned the US’s top envoy to Hong Kong after the consulate posted an alert about new rules giving authorities power to demand passwords for smartphones or other devices in national security investigations.

item.title: Leadership Row Erupts at Philippine Conglomerate Over ABS-CBN Capital Infusion
item.description: A leadership dispute within Lopez Inc., one of the Philippines’ biggest conglomerates, led to the ouster of its president over what he said was a disagreement over a 2-billion peso ($33 million) capital infusion for media unit ABS-CBN Corp.

item.title: Russia’s Ust-Luga Port Takes New Damage in Ukraine Drone Attack
item.description: Russia’s Ust-Luga port sustained fresh damage from a Ukrainian drone attack, as Kyiv steps up strikes on Russian oil export infrastructure.

item.title: Two Australia States to Offer Free Transport as Fuel Prices Rise
item.description: Two Australian states will temporarily offer free public transport to ease the effects of rising fuel costs, as prolonged hostilities in the Middle East increase supply risks.

item.title: New Delhi Street Stalls Show the Cost of India’s Energy Crunch
item.description: Outside New Delhi’s main bus terminus, a cluster of food stalls set up under makeshift canopies has been selling pan-fried flatbreads, comforting stews and hot tea to weary travelers for decades.

item.title: US Job Market Likely Thawed Out This Month After February Chill
item.description: US employment probably rebounded in March after one of the biggest pullbacks in payrolls since the pandemic, extending a string of volatile readings.

item.title: Inside ‘Project Eagle,’ JPMorgan’s High-Wire Act to Fund EA Deal
item.description: The post from @realDonaldTrump landed at precisely 7:23 a.m. Inside JPMorgan Chase & Co., the final piece of Project Eagle was a go.

item.title: Mexican Navy Locates Missing Aid Vessels Destined for Cuba
item.description: A Mexican Navy aircraft has located two small vessels that had gone missing while attempting to deliver aid to Cuba.

item.title: India Says Iran War Could Hit Growth, Widen Fiscal Deficit
item.description: India sees the Iran war weighing on growth and possibly widening its fiscal deficit as energy and shipping disruptions shortages ripple across sectors.

item.title: Zelenskiy Seeks Fuel Supply Deals During Middle East Tour
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said he’s using a Middle East trip to lock in lucrative long-term agreements on military cooperation and seek secure fuel supplies.

item.title: Emirates Global Aluminium Says Smelter Site Damaged in Attack
item.description: Emirates Global Aluminium said on Saturday that its Al Taweelah site sustained significant damage during Iranian missile and drone attacks at the Kezad industrial site in the emirate of Abu Dhabi.

item.title: Actors Union Is Bargaining for ‘Tilly Tax’ On AI Film Characters
item.description: As adoption of artificial intelligence in the US outpaces efforts to regulate it, organized labor is providing an important check on how the technology gets used, according to the head of the Hollywood actors’ union.

item.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Ships Exit Persian Gulf Through Iranian Corridor
item.description: A trickle of ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz along a route hugging the Iranian coastline as the war enters a second month. Only four vessels have been visible leaving the Persian Gulf over the past day.

item.title: Why Private Credit Is Facing a Sudden Investor Exodus
item.description: SaaSpocalypse. Credit defaults. The war in Iran. A lot of worries are hitting the $1.8 trillion private credit market at the same time, setting off a scramble by some investors to withdraw money from the industry’s giants. In recent weeks, funds managed by firms such as Apollo Global Management Inc., BlackRock Inc. and Ares Management Corp. have faced unprecedented requests for redemptions — and, in many cases, have exercised their right to block investors from getting all their money out.

item.title: Italy Energy Measures Won’t Break Fiscal Limits, Giorgetti Says
item.description: The Italian government’s aid to businesses and families aimed at protecting them from higher prices caused by the US war in Iran won’t damage the country’s fiscal position, Economy Minister Giancarlo Giorgetti said.

item.title: AI Schism Grips Washington as Tech, Labor Vie for Upper Hand
item.description: At a historic auditorium in Washington this week, Silicon Valley executives, Trump administration officials and members of Congress gathered in a sea of button-down shirts to extol the virtues of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Oil Surge Elicits Tough Love From Trump’s Latin American Allies
item.description: Latin American governments from Panama to Chile that politically aligned themselves with Donald Trump are now absorbing a hit from the global oil-price surge triggered by their US ally’s war on Iran.

item.title: The Iran War Is Testing US Alliances and Alarming Farmers
item.description: The effects of the conflict are widespread.

item.title: India Plans 100 Airports, 200 Helipads to Boost Connectivity
item.description: India plans to build 100 new airports and 200 helipads in smaller towns and cities in coming years as the government seeks to boost regional connectivity, trade and tourism.

item.title: Iran War Shockwaves Threaten More Damage to US Allies
item.description: From Europe to East Asia, the high price of the war may soon get even higher.

item.title: Another Two India-Bound LPG Tankers Exit Gulf Through Hormuz
item.description: Two more India-bound tankers carrying liquefied petroleum gas have exited the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz, adding to the trickle of vessels making it through the narrow waterway.

item.title: Iran War Leaves Global Economic Leaders Searching for Answers
item.description: The Iran war is forcing soul-searching within the global economic elite on how to respond to a series of shocks that show no signs of abating.
